items.q: What is reduction in terms of electrons?
items.correct: Gain of electrons (the oxidation number is reduced).
items.misconception: Reduction means losing oxygen and oxidation means gaining oxygen — full stop.
items.random_wrong: Reduction means a substance becomes physically smaller in size.
items.why: Students learn the oxygen definition first (from rusting/combustion) and never update to the more general electron-transfer definition.

items.q: When water boils, what is broken?
items.correct: Intermolecular forces (hydrogen bonds) between water molecules are broken — the O-H covalent bonds stay intact.
items.misconception: The covalent bonds between hydrogen and oxygen atoms are broken when water boils.
items.random_wrong: Water molecules split into hydrogen and oxygen gas when they boil.
items.why: Students don't distinguish between bonds within a molecule and forces between molecules, so they assume boiling 'breaks the molecule apart'.

items.q: Why are most transition metal complex ions coloured?
items.correct: Ligands split the d-orbitals into two energy levels; electrons absorb visible light to jump between them (d-d transitions), and the complementary colour is transmitted.
items.misconception: Transition metal ions are coloured because they have a half-full d-subshell which naturally absorbs light.
items.random_wrong: Transition metals are coloured because their nuclei emit photons in the visible range.
items.why: Students see 'd-block = coloured' as a label but miss that colour requires a partially filled d-subshell AND ligand-induced splitting — Sc3+ and Zn2+ are colourless because the d level is empty or full.

items.q: The oxidation state of Mn in MnO₄⁻ is:
items.why: Correct answer: +7. “+4” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: A cell has E°(cathode) = +0.80 V and E°(anode) = −0.40 V. The EMF is:
items.why: The answer examiners want is: +1.20 V. “+0.40 V” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.
